question: Does the proposed structure meet the level of detail required before beginning the South African provider universe?

Confirm whether the revised scope should keep close lease-like providers such as subscription-based leasing and commercial full-maintenance truck leasing, while excluding OEM captive finance arms, pure monthly rental brands and ecosystem-only providers.
status: Resolved - user requested removal of excluded providers

question: NedFleet has public evidence of full maintenance leasing and operating rentals, but its current market relationship with Nedbank/Eqstra should be validated before product extraction.
Review whether the Phase 2 product catalogue is complete enough for the 22 active providers before proceeding to detailed functionality extraction.
Some providers disclose only broad leasing/fleet-management products publicly. Should these medium-confidence records be retained for functionality extraction, or validated directly first?
Review whether the WesBank functionality entries have the right level of granularity before scaling Phase 3 across the remaining providers.

WesBank public pages are JavaScript-heavy and expose limited extractable text. Should we validate WesBank functionality detail through brochures/direct review before scaling inferred sub-capabilities?
Review the full functionality extraction across all 22 active South African providers before proceeding to Phase 4 capability standardisation.

Twenty functionality entries are marked Inferred because public provider wording supports the inclusion indirectly but does not disclose full mechanics. These should be validated before director-level claims are finalised.
A subagent reviewed functionality names, descriptions, why-included reasoning and evidence summaries. Weak or duplicate rows were removed and several overreaching functionality names were renamed.
Review the first capability dictionary with 32 standard functionalities and confirm or amend the terminology before proceeding to Phase 5 MaaS role classification.

Notable normalisations merged WesBank-specific labels such as Business Vehicle Leasing, Fleet Funding and Fuel Solutions into broader standard capabilities. Confirm that this level of normalisation is acceptable.
Review the MaaS category allocation before proceeding to Phase 6 functionality matrix views.
The classification agent proposed Fleet Operations Platform and Vehicle Lifecycle Platform as possible future categories. They were not added to maas_category and require user approval before use.
Review the matrix-ready views and confirm whether the South Africa matrix is ready before expanding to other African markets.
